name: Minecraft 32 Diamonds Fastest Strategy
description: Develop an evidence-based Minecraft Java 1.21.x strategy to obtain 32 diamonds as quickly as possible from surface start with iron-tier gear and allowed preparation/testing.

paths.name: Diamond route comparison path
paths.description: High-level model comparing direct cave access, branch mining, prep-first, and scout-hybrid approaches for time to 32 diamonds.

nodes.name: Start on surface with iron-tier gear

routes.name: Branch Mine at Y -58
routes.summary: Build a controlled strip/branch mine at the modern diamond band around Y -58 and systematically mine until 32 diamonds are reached.
routes.decisionReason: Best general-purpose and most reliable route to 32 diamonds in ordinary worlds.
routes.evidence: Search evidence repeatedly supports Y -58 / Y -59 as the diamond band, and branch mining is predictable with low variance.

routes.name: Deep Cave Rush
routes.summary: Reach the nearest deep cave system quickly and mine exposed diamond ore while staying around the optimal deepslate layer.
routes.decisionReason: Nearby deep caves can shortcut the descent and may beat pure mining in favorable spawns.
routes.evidence: Modern diamond strategy supports cave exposure as a viable fast method, and the player has adequate gear to exploit a nearby cave safely.

routes.name: Loot-and-Scout Hybrid
routes.summary: Use quick surface/structure loot and cave scouting to bootstrap tools, information, and possibly partial diamonds before committing to the best extraction method.
routes.decisionReason: Scout/loot detours add variance and are likely slower than immediate depth-focused execution.
routes.evidence: The target is small enough that detours rarely pay back, though scouting can be embedded as a substep if needed.

routes.name: Prep First: Fortune + Efficiency Setup
routes.summary: Spend early time obtaining an enchanted diamond pickaxe or better via villagers/anvils and then mine diamonds with Fortune to cut total ore required.
routes.decisionReason: Fortune/enchantment setup is too expensive for the small 32-diamond target unless a village chain is unusually free.
routes.evidence: Prep cost usually dominates the time budget, and evidence supports direct mining as the primary baseline.
